Step 3: Compare the two materials Material 1 has work function $\phi$. Material 2 has work function $2\phi$. solution: Step 1: Write the decay law The activity at time $t$ is given by: $ N(t) = N_0 e^{-\lambda t} $ where $\lambda$ is the decay constant. Step 2: Find decay constant from given data At $t=3$ min, $ N(3) = \frac{N_0}{e} $ So, $ \frac{N_0}{e} = N_0 e^{-3\lambda} $ Cancel $N_0$: $ \frac{1}{e} = e^{-3\lambda} $ $ e^{-1} = e^{-3\lambda} $ $ -1 = -3\lambda \quad \Rightarrow \quad \lambda = \tfrac{1}{3} $ So, the decay constant is: $ \lambda = \frac{1}{3}\,\text{per minute} $ Step 3: Find half-life The half-life is: $ T_{1/2} = \frac{\ln 2}{\lambda} $ Substitute $\lambda = \frac{1}{3}$: $ T_{1/2} = \frac{\ln 2}{1/3} = 3 \ln 2 $ Final Answer: $ \boxed{3 \ln 2} $ That corresponds to Option B ✅

solution: Step 1: Concept This is an application of Bernoulli’s principle. Wind flowing above the roof reduces the pressure on the outside relative to the inside (atmospheric pressure). The pressure difference produces a lift-type force on the roof. Bernoulli: $ P + \tfrac{1}{2}\rho v^2 = \text{constant} $ Inside, air is still (v ≈ 0), so pressure inside = atmospheric pressure = $P_\text{atm}$. Outside (on top of roof), wind has speed $50$, so pressure there is reduced by $ \Delta P = \tfrac{1}{2} \rho v^2 $ Step 2: Compute the pressure difference $ \Delta P = \tfrac{1}{2}(1.2)(50^2) $ $ = 0.6 \times 2500 = 1500\;\text{Pa} $ Step 3: Force on the roof $ F = \Delta P \times A = 1500 \times 300 = 4.5 \times 10^5 \;\text{N} $ Final Answer: The magnitude of the force exerted by the wind is: Option C: $4.5 \times 10^5\,\text{N}$ ✅
